# 5. KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

## 5.1 Part A Questions (3 Marks Each)

### **Q1. [KTU University Exam - May 2024]**
**Define the term "Balanced Transportation Problem." What condition must be satisfied for a TP to be balanced?**

**Model Answer:**

> A Transportation Problem is said to be **balanced** when the total supply (capacity of all sources) exactly equals the total demand (requirement of all destinations).

**Mathematical Condition:**

$$
\sum_{i=1}^{m} a_i = \sum_{j=1}^{n} b_j
$$

If this condition is not satisfied, a dummy row or dummy column with zero cost is added to convert it into a balanced TP before applying VAM or MODI. This ensures the formulation conforms to a closed, solvable linear programming model.

---

### **Q2. [KTU University Exam - Dec 2023]**
**State the formula for Economic Order Quantity (EOQ). Explain the meaning of each term.**

**Model Answer:**

$$
Q^* = \sqrt{\frac{2 D C_o}{C_h}}
$$

Where:
- **$Q^*$** = Optimal economic order quantity (units per order)
- **$D$** = Annual demand for the item (units/year)
- **$C_o$** = Fixed ordering cost per order (₹/order)
- **$C_h$** = Inventory holding/carrying cost per unit per year (₹/unit/year)

The formula is derived by minimizing the total annual variable cost, which is the sum of ordering cost ($D C_o / Q$) and holding cost ($Q C_h / 2$), with respect to $Q$.

---

## 5.2 Part B Questions (14 Marks) — Module Internal Choice

### **Question A: [KTU University Exam - May 2024, Module 4]**

**(a)** Solve the following Transportation Problem using **Vogel's Approximation Method (VAM)** to find an initial basic feasible solution. The supply at factories $O_1, O_2, O_3$ is **50, 40, 60** units, and demand at warehouses $D_1, D_2, D_3, D_4$ is **30, 30, 40, 50** units.

Cost Matrix:
$$
C = \begin{bmatrix} 5 & 1 & 3 & 3 \\ 3 & 4 & 6 & 4 \\ 2 & 5 & 4 & 6 \end{bmatrix}
$$

(7 Marks)

**(b)** Test the optimality of the above solution using the **MODI Method**. If not optimal, find the optimal transportation plan. (7 Marks)

---

**Model Solution:**

### Part (a) — VAM Solution

**Balance Check:** Supply = $50 + 40 + 60 = 150$. Demand = $30 + 30 + 40 + 50 = 150$. Balanced ✓

**Iteration 1:**
- Row penalties: R1 = $3-1=2$, R2 = $4-3=1$, R3 = $5-2=3$
- Col penalties: C1 = $3-2=1$, C2 = $4-1=3$, C3 = $4-3=1$, C4 = $4-3=1$
- Max penalty = **3** (R3). Min cost in R3 = $c_{31} = 2$. Allocate $\min(60, 30) = 30$. Demand $D_1$ exhausted.

**Iteration 2** (after removing Col 1): Remaining supply: 50, 40, 30. Demand: 30, 30, 40, 50.

Row penalties: R1 = $3-1=2$, R2 = $4-4=0$, R3 = $5-4=1$
Col penalties: C2 = $4-1=3$, C3 = $4-3=1$, C4 = $4-3=1$

Max penalty = **3** (C2). Min cost in C2 = $c_{12} = 1$. Allocate $\min(50, 30) = 30$. $D_2$ exhausted.

**Iteration 3** (after removing Col 2): Remaining supply: 20, 40, 30. Demand: 30, 40, 50.

Row penalties: R1 = $3-3=0$, R2 = $6-4=2$, R3 = $4-4=0$
Col penalties: C3 = $4-3=1$, C4 = $4-3=1$

Max penalty = **2** (R2). Min cost in R2 = $c_{24} = 4$. Allocate $\min(40, 50) = 40$. $O_2$ exhausted.

**Iteration 4:** Remaining: 20, 30. Demand: 30, 50.

Allocate $c_{13} = 3$: $\min(20, 30) = 20$. $O_1$ exhausted. Remaining 10 for $D_4$.

Allocate $c_{34} = 6$: $\min(30, 10) = 10$. $D_4$ partially filled. Remaining 20 for $D_3$.

Allocate $c_{33} = 4$: 20 units.

**Initial Allocation Matrix:**
$$
X = \begin{bmatrix} - & 30 & 20 & - \\ - & - & - & 40 \\ 30 & - & 20 & 10 \end{bmatrix}
$$

**Initial Cost:**
$$
Z_{VAM} = (1 \times 30) + (3 \times 20) + (4 \times 40) + (2 \times 30) + (4 \times 20) + (6 \times 10) = 30 + 60 + 160 + 60 + 80 + 60 = 450
$$

### Part (b) — MODI Optimality Test

Number of allocations = 6 = $(m + n - 1) = (3+4-1) = 6$ ✓

Setting $u_1 = 0$ and solving $u_i + v_j = c_{ij}$:
- $(1,2)$: $0 + v_2 = 1 \Rightarrow v_2 = 1$
- $(1,3)$: $0 + v_3 = 3 \Rightarrow v_3 = 3$
- $(2,4)$: $u_2 + v_4 = 4 \Rightarrow u_2 + v_4 = 4$
- $(3,1)$: $u_3 + v_1 = 2 \Rightarrow v_1 = 2 - u_3$
- $(3,3)$: $u_3 + 3 = 4 \Rightarrow u_3 = 1$
- $(3,1)$: $1 + v_1 = 2 \Rightarrow v_1 = 1$
- $(3,4)$: $1 + v_4 = 6 \Rightarrow v_4 = 5$
- $(2,4)$: $u_2 + 5 = 4 \Rightarrow u_2 = -1$

**Unoccupied Cells:**

| Cell | $c_{ij}$ | $u_i + v_j$ | $\Delta_{ij}$ |
|---|---|---|---|
| (1,1) | 5 | $0+1 = 1$ | **+4** |
| (1,4) | 3 | $0+5 = 5$ | **−2** ← Negative |
| (2,1) | 3 | $-1+1 = 0$ | **+3** |
| (2,2) | 4 | $-1+1 = 0$ | **+4** |
| (2,3) | 6 | $-1+3 = 2$ | **+4** |

Most negative $\Delta = -2$ at cell $(1,4)$. Closed loop: $(1,4) \to (1,3) \to (3,3) \to (3,4) \to (1,4)$.

Theta = $\min(20, 10) = 10$.

**New Allocation:**
$$
X_{opt} = \begin{bmatrix} - & 30 & 10 & 10 \\ - & - & - & 40 \\ 30 & - & 30 & - \end{bmatrix}
$$

New cost: $Z = (1 \times 30) + (3 \times 10) + (3 \times 10) + (4 \times 40) + (2 \times 30) + (4 \times 30) = 30 + 30 + 30 + 160 + 60 + 120 = 430$

$$
\boxed{Z_{\text{optimal}} = 430}
$$

---

### **Question B: [KTU University Exam - May 2024, Module 4 Alternative]**

**(a)** A project consists of 7 activities with the following details:

| Activity | Predecessor | $a$ | $m$ | $b$ |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1-2 | — | 3 | 6 | 15 |
| 1-3 | — | 2 | 5 | 14 |
| 2-4 | 1-2 | 6 | 7 | 8 |
| 2-5 | 1-2 | 5 | 8 | 11 |
| 3-5 | 1-3 | 4 | 7 | 10 |
| 4-6 | 2-4 | 3 | 4 | 5 |
| 5-6 | 2-5, 3-5 | 1 | 4 | 7 |

Determine:
(i) Expected time and variance for each activity. (3 Marks)
(ii) Identify the critical path and project completion time. (2 Marks)
(iii) Find the probability of project completion within **20 days**. (2 Marks)

**(b)** Compute the **Economic Order Quantity (EOQ)**, total cost, and number of orders per year for an item with annual demand $D = 8000$ units, ordering cost $C_o = ₹150$, holding cost $C_h = ₹6$ per unit per year. Also find the cycle time. (7 Marks)

---

**Model Solution:**

### Part (a) — PERT Analysis

**(i) Expected Time and Variance**

$$
t_{12} = \frac{3 + 4(6) + 15}{6} = \frac{42}{6} = 7, \quad \sigma_{12}^2 = \left(\frac{15-3}{6}\right)^2 = 4
$$

$$
t_{13} = \frac{2 + 4(5) + 14}{6} = \frac{36}{6} = 6, \quad \sigma_{13}^2 = \left(\frac{14-2}{6}\right)^2 = 4
$$

$$
t_{24} = \frac{6 + 4(7) + 8}{6} = \frac{42}{6} = 7, \quad \sigma_{24}^2 = \left(\frac{8-6}{6}\right)^2 = 0.111
$$

$$
t_{25} = \frac{5 + 4(8) + 11}{6} = \frac{48}{6} = 8, \quad \sigma_{25}^2 = \left(\frac{11-5}{6}\right)^2 = 1
$$

$$
t_{35} = \frac{4 + 4(7) + 10}{6} = \frac{42}{6} = 7, \quad \sigma_{35}^2 = \left(\frac{10-4}{6}\right)^2 = 1
$$

$$
t_{46} = \frac{3 + 4(4) + 5}{6} = \frac{24}{6} = 4, \quad \sigma_{46}^2 = \left(\frac{5-3}{6}\right)^2 = 0.111
$$

$$
t_{56} = \frac{1 + 4(4) + 7}{6} = \frac{24}{6} = 4, \quad \sigma_{56}^2 = \left(\frac{7-1}{6}\right)^2 = 1
$$

**(ii) Critical Path**

Paths and durations:
- 1-2-4-6: $7 + 7 + 4 = 18$ days
- 1-2-5-6: $7 + 8 + 4 = 19$ days
- 1-3-5-6: $6 + 7 + 4 = 17$ days

**Critical Path: 1-2-5-6**, Expected Duration $T_E = 19$ days.

Critical Path Variance: $\sigma_{CP}^2 = 4 + 1 + 1 = 6$, $\sigma_{CP} = 2.449$

**(iii) Probability of Completion within 20 days**

$$
Z = \frac{T_s - T_E}{\sigma_{CP}} = \frac{20 - 19}{2.449} = 0.408
$$

From standard normal tables, $P(Z \leq 0.41) \approx 0.6591$.

$$
\boxed{P(\text{Completion} \leq 20 \text{ days}) \approx 65.91\%}
$$

### Part (b) — EOQ Calculation

$$
Q^* = \sqrt{\frac{2 D C_o}{C_h}} = \sqrt{\frac{2 \times 8000 \times 150}{6}} = \sqrt{400000} = 632.46 \text{ units}
$$

$$
N^* = \frac{D}{Q^*} = \frac{8000}{632.46} = 12.65 \approx 13 \text{ orders/year}
$$

$$
T^* = \frac{1}{N^*} = \frac{632.46}{8000} = 0.079 \text{ years} \approx 29 \text{ days}
$$

$$
TC^* = \sqrt{2 D C_o C_h} = \sqrt{2 \times 8000 \times 150 \times 6} = \sqrt{14400000} = ₹3794.73
$$

---

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning — Common Pitfalls:**
> 1. **Assignment Problems:** Students often forget to perform **column reduction** after row reduction — losing 1–2 marks.
> 2. **MODI Method:** Always check the count of allocations = $(m+n-1)$. If not, the basis is **degenerate** — assign $\epsilon$ to satisfy non-degeneracy.
> 3. **PERT:** A common mistake is averaging all activity variances instead of summing variances only along the **critical path** for Z-score computation.
> 4. **EOQ:** Holding cost must be in **per unit per year**; convert weekly/monthly figures consistently.
> 5. **Queuing:** Never apply $L = \lambda W$ formula for non-Poisson arrival systems.

---

## 5.3 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember

> [!NOTE]
> **Rapid Revision Checklist — Module 4 Applications**

- ☐ **Transportation Problem**: Balanced TP requires $\sum a_i = \sum b_j$. Use VAM for initial BFS, MODI for optimality test.
- ☐ **MODI Optimality**: All $\Delta_{ij} \geq 0$ at optimality. Most negative $\Delta_{ij}$ enters the basis.
- ☐ **Hungarian Method**: Apply row reduction, then column reduction, then cover zeros — minimum lines = $n$ indicates optimality.
- ☐ **Maximization AP**: Convert via $c'_{ij} = \max(c) - c_{ij}$ before applying Hungarian.
- ☐ **PERT Expected Time**: $t_e = (a + 4m + b)/6$ — Beta Distribution approximation.
- ☐ **PERT Variance**: $\sigma^2 = ((b - a)/6)^2$. **Sum variances along critical path only**.
- ☐ **CPM Critical Path**: Path with zero slack. Earliest = forward pass, Latest = backward pass.
- ☐ **EOQ Formula**: $Q^* = \sqrt{2DC_o/C_h}$ — assumes constant demand, no shortages.
- ☐ **EOQ Total Cost**: $TC^* = \sqrt{2DC_oC_h}$ (minimum).
- ☐ **M/M/1 Stability**: $\rho = \lambda/\mu < 1$. Else queue is unstable.
- ☐ **Little's Law**: $L = \lambda W$ applies to **any** queuing system in steady-state.
- ☐ **LIFO / FCFS**: FCFS is default in KTU exam unless otherwise specified.
- ☐ **KTU Module 4 Weightage**: Carries **14 marks** in Part B with internal choice, plus 3-mark Part A questions.

> **Master Equation for the Module:**
> $$\boxed{\text{Real Problem} \xrightarrow{\text{Model}} \text{Math Form} \xrightarrow{\text{Algorithm}} \text{Optimal Decision}}$$
